issues.code:
1 import urllib.request
2 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
3 import yaml
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

issues.code:
1 import urllib.request
2 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
3 import yaml
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
issues.judge_reason: Importing xml.etree.ElementTree without defusedxml leaves the code vulnerable to XML attacks; confirms B314.

issues.code:
6     try:
7         response = urllib.request.urlopen(xml_url, timeout=5)
8         xml_data = response.read()
issues.description: Audit url open for permitted schemes. Allowing use of file:/ or custom schemes is often unexpected.
issues.judge_reason: urlopen is called with user-controlled xml_url without restricting allowed schemes; file:// or other schemes could be used.

issues.code:
8         xml_data = response.read()
9         root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)

issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: ET.fromstring does not disable DTD/external entities by default; vulnerable to XXE attacks.
Bare except: pass silently swallows all exceptions, hiding errors and making debugging impossible.
